message:
br-bd-5lxot implement renameat2 RENAME_EXCHANGE atomic dual-entry swap

Pre-fix bd-bivg2 shipped RENAME_NOREPLACE; the EXCHANGE leg was left
returning EINVAL with a TODO. This finishes the contract: same-parent
and cross-parent swaps both work for ext4. btrfs still returns EINVAL
(its COW dir-item layout needs a separate path).

ffs_dir::swap_inode_in_entry:
- New primitive that finds the live entry whose name matches and
  rewrites only the leading 4-byte inode field, leaving rec_len,
  name_len, file_type tag, and the name bytes untouched.
- Tombstones (inode == 0) are skipped so a deleted entry that happens
  to share the name is never matched.
- Validates the layout (rec_len bounds, name_len in bounds) on every
  hop and returns FfsError::Corruption rather than panicking on a
  malformed dir block.
- Returns Ok(false) when the name is absent so the caller can iterate
  multiple dir blocks.

OpenFs::ext4_rename2_exchange (in the impl OpenFs block):
- Lookups both source and destination — either missing -> ENOENT.
- Pulls the dir extents for parent + new_parent and walks each in
  turn, calling swap_inode_in_entry to retarget. Same-parent reuses
  the parent_extents Vec; cross-parent fetches both.
- If the destination swap fails after the source swap committed, the
  source side is rolled back (best-effort) and the caller surfaces
  Corruption — fsck-grade recovery is the contract.
- Touches ctime on both swapped inodes per the renameat2(2) man page;
  mtime, atime, nlink, mode all stay.

OpenFs::rename2 routes RENAME_EXCHANGE through the new helper instead
of returning EINVAL. The existing same-name same-parent fast path is
preserved as a successful no-op.

Tests: 676 ffs-core --lib pass (was 672 + 4 net new — the existing
EINVAL-until-implemented test became a positive same-parent regression,
plus four new ones). New regressions:
- exchange_swaps_two_entries_in_same_parent: distinct payloads X/Y;
  after RENAME_EXCHANGE, lookup ex_x.txt returns y_ino + Y data,
  lookup ex_y.txt returns x_ino + X data.
- exchange_swaps_across_distinct_parents: dir_a/x and dir_b/y end up
  swapped across parents.
- exchange_missing_source_returns_enoent.
- exchange_missing_target_returns_enoent.
- exchange_same_name_same_parent_is_noop preserves the inode.

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_5lxot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
